issue desc: The security configuration permits all requests via anyRequest().permitAll(), leaving every controller endpoint reachable without framework-enforced authentication.
The admin LDAP search endpoint has no authorization check, so any caller can query directory user information.

CWE name: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
issue desc: The user listing endpoint returns usernames, email addresses, roles, and statuses without any authentication or authorization check.

The document download and upload endpoints authorize only by publicRef in the URL and perform no caller authentication, enabling unauthorized read or overwrite of another user's document if the reference is known.
The application configuration contains hard-coded secrets: the notification API key and the LDAP bind username/password.
The notification gateway is configured with an HTTP base URL, so the API key and MFA SMS payloads would be sent without transport encryption.
The H2 database console is enabled, exposing a development-only administrative interface if deployed with this configuration.
The startup initializer creates default user accounts with a fixed known password, introducing built-in credentials.
Password reset tokens are stored directly in plaintext in the database instead of as one-way verifiers.
The MFA one-time passcode is stored in plaintext in persistent storage.
